name: Lake Victoria
description: Lake Victoria is the largest lake in Africa by surface area, a vital freshwater lake shared by Kenya, Uganda, and Tanzania. It supports diverse aquatic life and is a significant source of livelihood for fishing communities.

name: Lake Turkana
key_feature: Largest desert lake; rich in fossils
description: Lake Turkana is the world's largest permanent desert lake, located in the Great Rift Valley of northern Kenya. Known for its jade-coloured waters and significant fossil discoveries, it holds UNESCO World Heritage Site status.

name: Lake Naivasha
key_feature: Freshwater lake with hippos and islands
description: Lake Naivasha is a freshwater lake in the Great Rift Valley, northwest of Nairobi, known for abundant birdlife and hippos. It is a popular tourist destination and supports an important flower-farming industry.

name: Lake Nakuru
key_feature: Renowned for flamingo flocks; national park
description: Lake Nakuru is a shallow, alkaline-saline lake in the Rift Valley, famous for its large flocks of flamingos and protected within Lake Nakuru National Park. It is also a key conservation area for both black and white rhinos.

name: Lake Baringo
key_feature: Freshwater Rift Valley lake with crocodiles
description: Lake Baringo is one of only two freshwater lakes in Kenya's Rift Valley, home to diverse bird species, hippos, and crocodiles. It plays a crucial role in local livelihoods through fishing and tourism.

name: Lake Bogoria
key_feature: Hot springs and geysers; flamingo populations
description: Lake Bogoria is a saline, alkaline lake in the Rift Valley famous for its geothermal geysers, hot springs, and large populations of lesser flamingos. It is recognised as a Ramsar wetland of international importance.

name: Lake Elementaita
key_feature: Alkaline soda lake; important bird sanctuary
description: Lake Elementaita is a soda lake in the Great Rift Valley between Lake Naivasha and Lake Nakuru, serving as a major breeding ground for pelicans and a feeding ground for flamingos. It forms part of a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Lake Magadi
key_feature: Highly saline soda lake; commercial trona mining
description: Lake Magadi is a saline, alkaline lake in the southern Rift Valley, known for its vast soda ash deposits, which are commercially extracted. Its harsh environment supports specialised flamingo populations.

name: Lake Jipe
description: Lake Jipe is a small, shallow lake on the Kenya-Tanzania border, serving as an important water source for wildlife within Tsavo West National Park and home to diverse bird species and crocodiles.

name: Lake Chala
key_feature: Crater lake with deep clear waters
description: Lake Chala is a freshwater crater lake on the Kenya-Tanzania border, fed by underground springs and known for its clear blue waters. It offers scenic swimming and kayaking opportunities.

name: Lake Logipi
key_feature: Shallow saline lake with hot springs
description: Lake Logipi is a temporary, saline, alkaline lake in the northern Rift Valley, located between Lake Bogoria and Lake Turkana. It serves as an important breeding ground for flamingos when water levels permit.

name: Lake Oloidien
description: Lake Oloidien is a small, freshwater-to-alkaline lake adjacent to Lake Naivasha, separated by a narrow land bridge. It is a significant habitat for flamingos and pelicans.

name: Lake Sonachi
key_feature: Small volcanic crater lake with dramatic cliffs
description: Lake Sonachi is a small, alkaline crater lake located within Hell's Gate National Park, known for its high mineral content and picturesque volcanic setting.

name: Lake Simbi Nyaima
key_feature: Legendary crater lake with clear waters
description: Lake Simbi Nyaima is a small, alkaline crater lake in western Kenya, surrounded by local legends and known for its distinct water chemistry and cultural significance.

name: Lake Ol Bolossat
key_feature: Central Kenya freshwater lake; hippos and birds
description: Lake Ol Bolossat is the only high-altitude freshwater lake in Kenya's central highlands, an important water source for surrounding communities and wildlife, and home to diverse bird species.

name: Lake Kanyaboli
key_feature: Freshwater oxbow lake; refuge for endemic fish
description: Lake Kanyaboli is a freshwater lake in western Kenya, part of the Yala Swamp system. It serves as a critical refuge for endangered fish species endemic to the Lake Victoria basin.

name: Lake Wamithi
description: Lake Wamithi is a small, seasonal freshwater lake located in central Kenya, important for local agriculture and as a stopover point for migratory birds during wet seasons.

name: Lake Sare
key_feature: Shallow satellite lake of Lake Victoria
description: Lake Sare is a shallow, freshwater lake in western Kenya connected to the Yala Swamp, serving as an important fishing ground supporting diverse aquatic flora and fauna for local communities.
